metadata.title: AI-Generated Code Vulnerability Tracker
metadata.description: Cataloging vulnerabilities specifically introduced or amplified by AI code generation tools, with real-world CVE mappings
metadata.methodology: Vulnerabilities sourced from academic research, CVE databases, vendor security advisories, and independent audits. Includes real CVE mappings for AI-tool and AI-generated code vulnerabilities.

ai_tool_vulnerabilities.description: Improper neutralization of special elements in GitHub Copilot allows unauthorized command execution through crafted prompts
LLM scope violation in Microsoft Copilot allows zero-click data theft via email-based prompt injection

ai_tool_vulnerabilities.type: File Protection Bypass via Case Sensitivity
ai_tool_vulnerabilities.description: Case-sensitivity mismatch in Cursor allows attackers to bypass file protections and modify configuration files
Attacker can alter MCP configuration behavior after user approval within Cursor, enabling remote code execution
Critical vulnerability chain in Langflow AI agent platform enables account takeover and remote code execution

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models frequently generate shell command constructions using unsanitized user input
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Input validation; subprocess with shell=False; parameterized commands

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated database queries frequently use string concatenation instead of parameterized queries
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Parameterized queries; ORM usage; SQL injection detection in SAST

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models generate HTML rendering code without proper output encoding or CSP headers
AI models trained on public repos reproduce hardcoded API keys, passwords, and tokens
vulnerability_categories.research_source: Niu et al., CodexLeaks, USENIX Security 2023
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Secret scanning in CI/CD; environment variable enforcement; git-secrets hooks

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models suggest MD5, SHA-1, DES, or ECB mode encryption instead of modern alternatives
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Crypto linting rules; SAST rules blocking weak algorithms

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated file handling code often lacks path canonicalization and directory boundary checks
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Path canonicalization; chroot/sandbox; allowlist-based file access

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models suggest pickle.load(), yaml.load() without safe loaders
AI-generated API endpoints frequently lack authorization middleware
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Authorization middleware; RBAC frameworks; API gateway policies

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated C/C++ code frequently omits null checks after memory allocation
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Static analysis; smart pointers in C++; nullable annotations

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models generate verbose error handling exposing stack traces and internal paths
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Custom error handlers; structured logging; environment-aware responses

vulnerability_categories.description: AI models generating C/C++ code produce buffer operations without bounds checking
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Bounds checking; safe string functions; AddressSanitizer in CI

vulnerability_categories.description: AI-generated HTTP client code passes user-supplied URLs without validation
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: URL allowlisting; network segmentation; SSRF-specific WAF rules

vulnerability_categories.description: AI tools themselves vulnerable to code injection via prompt injection attacks
vulnerability_categories.mitigation: Sandboxed execution; MCP configuration review; prompt injection defenses

aggregate_statistics.note: Version 2.0 adds real CVE mappings and AI-tool-specific vulnerability tracking. Prevalence scores based on independent security evaluations.
